---
title: 'Female Orgasms, G-Spot, and the Clit'
date: '2024-07-10T14:28:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'The Dirty Pastor'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '7138328774232489967'
blogger_filename: '/2024/07/female-orgasms-g-spot-and-clit.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/The Dirty Pastor/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Sex'
  - 'Female Orgasms'
---

<h3 style="text-align: left;">A frank, blunt, clinical level discussion of&nbsp;Female Orgasms, G-Spot, and the Clit</h3><p><b><i><br /></i></b></p><p><b><i><br /></i></b></p><p><b><i style="background-color: #fcff01;">Article Under Construction...&nbsp;</i></b></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/vaginal-anatomy-a5ed43ea77f2.png" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt="Illustration by Cristie Wilson, via Healthline Website" border="0" data-original-height="967" data-original-width="1296" height="478" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/vaginal-anatomy-1c8c335fe73b.png" title="Illustration by Cristie Wilson, via Healthline Website" width="640" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://www.healthline.com/health/healthy-sex/clitoris#clitoral-anatomy" target="_blank">Illustration by Cristie Wilson, via Healthline Website</a></td></tr></tbody></table><p></p><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><span style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><a href="https://centerforeroticintelligence.org/the-internal-clitoris/" target="_blank"><img alt="Mal Harrison, Center for Erotic Intelligence" border="0" data-original-height="417" data-original-width="1024" height="260" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/vaginal-anatomy-2-485684671fd8.jpg" title="Mal Harrison, Center for Erotic Intelligence" width="640" /></a></span></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://centerforeroticintelligence.org/the-internal-clitoris/" target="_blank">Mal Harrison, Center for Erotic Intelligence</a></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div>I've been meaning to write this post for a few years. I have so many side-quest projects that this one has taken a back-burner. However, a recent post <a href="https://www.threads.net/@doctortammi/post/C9N5qb-SnFr?xmt=AQGzF_r3oxthyrLIvK3OyP4cFcUJiIV6B4wWQtlllHLg6A" target="_blank">on Threads by @DoctorTammi</a> prompted a sudden burst of inspiration. I am taking my initial comments and turning them into a post here, which I will now expand upon.</div><div><br />
<blockquote class="text-post-media" data-text-post-permalink="https://www.threads.net/@doctortammi/post/C9N5qb-SnFr" data-text-post-version="0" id="ig-tp-C9N5qb-SnFr" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.15); border-radius: 16px; border-style: solid; border-width: 1px; margin: 1px; max-width: 540px; min-width: 270px; padding: 0px; width: calc(100% - 2px);"> <a href="https://www.threads.net/@doctortammi/post/C9N5qb-SnFr" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; line-height: 0; padding: 0px; text-align: center; text-decoration: none; width: 100%;" target="_blank"> <div style="align-items: center; display: flex; flex-direction: column; padding: 40px;"><div style="display: block; height: 32px; padding-bottom: 20px; width: 32px;"> <svg aria-label="Threads" height="32px" role="img" viewbox="0 0 192 192" width="32px" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"> <path d="M141.537 88.9883C140.71 88.5919 139.87 88.2104 139.019 87.8451C137.537 60.5382 122.616 44.905 97.5619 44.745C97.4484 44.7443 97.3355 44.7443 97.222 44.7443C82.2364 44.7443 69.7731 51.1409 62.102 62.7807L75.881 72.2328C81.6116 63.5383 90.6052 61.6848 97.2286 61.6848C97.3051 61.6848 97.3819 61.6848 97.4576 61.6855C105.707 61.7381 111.932 64.1366 115.961 68.814C118.893 72.2193 120.854 76.925 121.825 82.8638C114.511 81.6207 106.601 81.2385 98.145 81.7233C74.3247 83.0954 59.0111 96.9879 60.0396 116.292C60.5615 126.084 65.4397 134.508 73.775 140.011C80.8224 144.663 89.899 146.938 99.3323 146.423C111.79 145.74 121.563 140.987 128.381 132.296C133.559 125.696 136.834 117.143 138.28 106.366C144.217 109.949 148.617 114.664 151.047 120.332C155.179 129.967 155.42 145.8 142.501 158.708C131.182 170.016 117.576 174.908 97.0135 175.059C74.2042 174.89 56.9538 167.575 45.7381 153.317C35.2355 139.966 29.8077 120.682 29.6052 96C29.8077 71.3178 35.2355 52.0336 45.7381 38.6827C56.9538 24.4249 74.2039 17.11 97.0132 16.9405C119.988 17.1113 137.539 24.4614 149.184 38.788C154.894 45.8136 159.199 54.6488 162.037 64.9503L178.184 60.6422C174.744 47.9622 169.331 37.0357 161.965 27.974C147.036 9.60668 125.202 0.195148 97.0695 0H96.9569C68.8816 0.19447 47.2921 9.6418 32.7883 28.0793C19.8819 44.4864 13.2244 67.3157 13.0007 95.9325L13 96L13.0007 96.0675C13.2244 124.684 19.8819 147.514 32.7883 163.921C47.2921 182.358 68.8816 191.806 96.9569 192H97.0695C122.03 191.827 139.624 185.292 154.118 170.811C173.081 151.866 172.51 128.119 166.26 113.541C161.776 103.087 153.227 94.5962 141.537 88.9883ZM98.4405 129.507C88.0005 130.095 77.1544 125.409 76.6196 115.372C76.2232 107.93 81.9158 99.626 99.0812 98.6368C101.047 98.5234 102.976 98.468 104.871 98.468C111.106 98.468 116.939 99.0737 122.242 100.233C120.264 124.935 108.662 128.946 98.4405 129.507Z"></path></svg></div> <div style="color: #999999; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 21px; padding-bottom: 4px;"> Post by @doctortammi</div> <div style="color: black; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 21px;"> View on Threads</div></div></a></blockquote>
<script async="" src="https://www.threads.net/embed.js"></script>

</div><div><br /></div><div>Here is my longer-form answer and expanded content...</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><h4 style="text-align: left;">Does the G-Spot Exist????</h4><div><br /></div><div>Yes, but, it isn't what most men, and even a lot of women, imagine it is. It's not magic, it's science, and it's fairly easy to work with once you understand the science.</div><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: left;"><tbody><tr><td class="tr-caption"><div><div>I scrolled through and some answers were included part of this. But I'll add to this thread. I've taught a lot of couples this information.</div><div>Men (and women) partners, especially those with ADHD, this is your new fidget toy.</div><div><br /></div><div>Many I've worked with imagine it's some elusive magic spot, and even have trouble finding it when doing exploration for it. You need to understand some basics about the anatomy. Humans start with a generic sexless set of reproductive organs. The two round structures either become Testes in male or ovaries in female.</div><div><br /></div><div>The longer structure with two tubes for blood and other similar structures become a penis in males.</div><div><br /></div><h4>CLITORIS</h4><div>The same blood tubes become a large wishbone structure in female. At the top of this structure is the clitoris head (the only part people usually talk about when they say Clit, and even that seems elusive for men who can't be bothered to do ten minutes of research. The rest wraps around and down. The entire structure works similar to the penis, becoming engorged during arousal.</div><div><br /></div><div>If you work your fingers, tongue, toys, penis, etc., in, around, against, and besides this structure at any point, you will have different zones of orgasm potential.However, if you go in cold, you'll get much less of any reaction. This is like an unaroused penis. It's not nothing, but it usually becomes more sensitive as it is engaged.</div><div><br /></div><div>Therefore, a significant amount of attention must be paid to the entire body, especially breasts, and brain (yes men, I said brain, she needs to want you and this, which is why romance books do for her what porn does for you)... The body and mind need to be aroused. Even after initial arousal, you will find it comes in stages. At each stage you will find different zones around this wishbone become sensitized, and create varying degrees of response for her.</div><div><br /></div><h4>G-SPOT</h4><div>It is only at this final stage when the "G-Spot" becomes a game changer. You can find this by placing one (or two ) fingers inside, up, and behind the area where the "Clit" is on the outside. You are essentially pulling against the nerve bundle on the backside of the same Clit you see on the outside.You should feel a muscle and bone wall that you pull back against, gentle but with increasing firmness as she becomes more aroused, the firmness depends on her particular sensitivity.</div><div><br /></div><div>For many, a pumping action, pulling back against this area, release slightly, pull again is the appropriate motion. Some people call this "come hither", but work together to see what firmness levels she likes. I have found a light tickle is NOT the right method usually, which is why I think "come hither" gives guys the wrong idea.</div><div><br /></div><div>For some, a firm pull up and back against this area and then holding it for a solid 30-60 seconds is a breath taking experience leading to multiple orgasms. Imagine holding her like you might a case of soda, actually pulling her almost off the bed (or seat). However, for some, this is too much and only hurts, experiment with her permission</div><div><br /></div><div>You may find this area squishy and formless before arousal, and then, just like a penis, it becomes shaped during arousal. You may find that eventually there is a pad of muscle and skin developing in this area. Essentially the nerve bundle creates a region to target during arousal. It may feel like squishy pad or fidget toy</div><div><br /></div><div>You may find additional areas around the vaginal opening and behind each wall that hold similar nerve clusters which can be activated differently during different stages of arousal, many of these regions could but may not lead some women to different kinds of orgasms in the same session</div><div><br /></div><h4>A-SPOT</h4><div>For some, only some, this wishbone may extend all the way down and around the vaginal cavity. You may, or may not, find there are nerve clusters at the very bottom, exactly the opposite of the G-Spot.In this case, the exact same two fingers in, back, and then forward against the aroused walls will have a similar affect, however, you are pulling towards her anal opening but from inside the vaginal cavity. For a special few, this may be even more intense than the G-Spot. Call it an A-spot?</div><div><br /></div><div>In rare cases, with two very open partners, two fingers inside this lower A-spot and a thumb inside the anus, pulling together and rubbing , touching your fingers to your thumb with her anal and vaginal cavity between, can send the woman's eyes rolling back into her head. But she has to be physically built with those nerve cluster and mentally open to the experience.</div><div><br /></div><div>This A-Spot region is going to vary wildly from woman to woman, with some finding it a very negative experience, and yet others finding it the single best option, even over and above external clit or internal G-Spot stimulation. You would need to have open, honest, and safe communication around this before, during, and after attempting this.</div><div><br /></div><h4>BODIES ARE DIFFERENT</h4><div>The human body has general structures it creates with DNA, which is why doctors can name certain veins and arteries because they are common to most humans, and missing one of these is a sign of abnormality. However, humans also vary from human to human, especially in the unique expression of smaller structures. While most cis-women will have the general structures in the image I provided, every woman may have her unique nerve clusters at different points in the cavity and different points of her arousal state.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div></div><i><br />Note: Experiment with pressure, rhythm, intensity, etc. Men, often when she gets excited we want to start rushing going faster, deeper, harder. This is almost always the WRONG choice. You want that because your orgasm requires it. Hers needs steady. Once you find the intensity, pressure, and pace she wants, DO NOT CHANGE unless she asks you to do so. Think of this as a heart beat, pump, pump, pump in a steady and consistent motion.<br /><br /></i><h4>COMMUNICATION</h4><div>To take advantage of these to the fullest capacity, you will need to communicate early, openly and often.</div><div><br /></div><div>It will require two partners who are open, honest, providing safety for each other to experiment in order to take full advantage of these arousal zones.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Both partners need to communicate about which areas are creating which responses. Iâ€™ve had too many partners to admit out loud, and Iâ€™ve never seen two identical ones. You have to communicate about what is working for this particular human.</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><h4>SAFE SPACE</h4><div>Men, that means no rushing, take your time, ask her feedback but also watch her, listen to her breathing, this requires some finesse. Marathon not sprint. This also requires she feels SAFE with you. If youâ€™ve been arguing all day, or dismissing her emotions, or even worse making her feel actively unsafe, forget all of this, none of it will work for you.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Women, unless heâ€™s been in counseling, he wonâ€™t tell you this, but your man needs safety too. If he is a good man, he wants to â€œperformâ€ for you. He wants to do a good job. He might even want to hear heâ€™s such a good boy! Like a puppy. lol But ultimately, he needs you to make it safe for him to get it wrong. Encourage him, give him instructions, correct his motions with encouragement and affirmation. Make it safe for him to try new techniques.</div><div><br /></div><div>It is highly unlikely you will discover everything there is to know about your female partner in one session. Communication and experimentation, with an understanding of the anatomy discussed here and in the image provided, will be key to discovering which erogenous zones are working for this particular human person.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div><i></i></div><i><br />Disclosure: It occurs to me that much of this post assumes a cis-hetero couple. That is partly because the invitation began with â€œI really want to hear from the men on this one.â€, and I am in fact a male man, and I am discussing standard biological and mental differences. Many partners will be in some other form on the LGBTQ+ spectrum, I think most of this should still apply. Iâ€™ve found many women didnâ€™t even know these things about their own body. So lesbian and non-binary folks should still learn something and find it useful, I believe.<br /><br /></i><div>While I could probably go on for many more pages, I think thatâ€™s enough for a Thread. Go experiment with your or your partnerâ€™s vaginal region today.  Shalom! Happy Orgasms.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div><i>End Thread /// I will come back and augment this article as time permits, but I wanted to at least have something published as it comes up a lot. // TBC...</i></div></div><div style="text-align: left;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: left;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: left;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: left;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: left;"><br /></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
